title: Include directives should not rely on non-portable search strategy
description: Include directives should not rely on non-portable search strategyAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoMicrosoft’s MSVC has a search strategy which differs from other compilers when resolving quoted include directives#include "file.h". Relying on such a strategy is not portable and may lead to compilation failure when trying to build with a different compiler.This rule raises an issue whenever the file specified in a#includedirective can only be found using the MSVC search strategy.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Include directive uses non-portable path that may only work with MSVC. Use portable relative paths or configure include directories properly.
Include path uses system-specific conventions that break portability across different operating systems and compilers.
Include directive relies on Microsoft MSVC-specific search behavior that will fail on other compilers.
